name: model-scope
description: Hand it a paper and get a simulator: /model-scope:from-paper reads ONLY the reference material, INTERVIEWS you about the visualization purpose and specific requests, then builds the interactive simulation GUI and serves it at a verified local URL (optional shareable tunnel or permanent deploy when tooling and auth allow). Or turn ANY parameterised model into an interactive explorer: move a slider and watch the simulation update, in views the MODEL defines (no fixed graphic or axis). Philosophy: replicate a model faithfully, illuminate it from the angles that fit its class, and let the user compare what qualitative and quantitative differences arise from the parameter and model choices (it is not a fixed pipeline, and a model with structure shows that structure first). A lens switch shows the class-appropriate angles: a decision model gets one trial and many-trial statistics; a network gets structure, activity, representation, and an energy landscape; an image/CNN model gets architecture then layer transforms; an observer gets measurement, likelihood, posterior, and metrics. Idioms: a continuous playhead, a process-mode stage stepper, and the lens switch. Build a whole paper as a few screens (structure, condition comparisons, prediction); heavy screens stream via runChunks with a loading overlay. Schema-driven sliders and toggles, a simulate-to-views contract, a small plotting helper, no build step. Includes a modelbook of canonical families grounded in real open source, a reusable code library, and a GUI QC pipeline.
